relevance: Foundational CPR genomes; describes unusual biology (self-splicing introns, unusual ribosomes) across >15% of bacterial diversity — baseline for what CPR genomes encode.
The tree of life placing CPR/Patescibacteria as a major bacterial radiation; phylogenetic framing.
~8,000 MAGs substantially expanding CPR sampling; the genome resource any census would draw on.
CPR members functionally uniform, generally obligate fermenters; little functional diversity — context for reduced regulatory needs.
Minimal translation apparatus in Mollicutes (Mycoplasma) under massive genome reduction; core RNA machinery retained.
The tiniest genomes (Carsonella, Nasuia); reference on extreme endosymbiont reduction.
Comparative genomics of small prokaryotic genomes; free-living vs endosymbiont reduction mechanisms.
Conserved small RNAs widely expressed in a small symbiont genome (Buchnera-type) — sRNA regulation persists in reduced genomes; Hfq status is the open comparative question.
Chance and necessity in endosymbiont genome evolution; gene-loss trajectories.
